title: Spiropetalum heterophyllum
taxonomy: Spiropetalum heterophyllum is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the gentian family and the coffee family. Further classification places Spiropetalum heterophyllum within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, a subfamily within the Apocynaceae family. This subfamily is primarily composed of tropical plants found in Africa and Asia. Additionally, Spiropetalum heterophyllum is a monotypic genus, meaning it is the sole member of its genus.

intro: Spiropetalum heterophyllum, commonly known as "Rouge de Fer" or "Bloodroot", is a plant species that belongs to the family Berberidaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Spiropetalum heterophyllum include alkaloids, such as berberine, and isoquinoline compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
